plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Add to QQQ, targeting 20%, to boost broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect reduced volatility but also lower potential for outsized gains.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to being below its stop and to reduce leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 12% to manage single name concentration.
Buy AMD, targeting 5%, based on its strong scanner setup.
Slightly increase QQQI to 20% for balanced income exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding a high-quality scanner setup. Expect moderate volatility and growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SMH to 10% due to its downtrend status.
Add to SPAL, targeting 5%, to increase leveraged ETF exposure.
Buy AMD, targeting 10%, based on its strong scanner setup.
Add to CRWD, targeting 15%, to press a winning position.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, leaning into stretch growth. It uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay and rebalancing, leading to potential rapid losses if stops are not strictly followed.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD to exit high-beta single name exposure.
Sell SMH to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
Add significantly to QQQI, targeting 40%, to maximize income exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented exposure.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown by rotating into income ETFs. Remember, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
